description: In Swift, a clean build does not prevent force-unwrap crashes on nil, retain cycles, and data races flagged by strict concurrency.
body: In Swift, passing the type checker, where optionals and protocol conformances must resolve says nothing about runtime: force-unwrap crashes on nil, retain cycles, and data races flagged by strict concurrency surface only when the code actually runs. Use when a change touches control flow, IO, concurrency, or data shapes that the build cannot prove safe. Do not use a green compile as evidence that behavior is correct, and avoid shipping without exercising the path. Actions: run swift test (XCTest/swift-testing) on the affected target, reproduce the runtime path, and where relevant run Instruments leaks and ARC retain-cycle inspection to catch latent defects. Validation signals include the test/trace logs, a clean Instruments leaks and ARC retain-cycle inspection run, and observed correct output on representative input. Risk boundary: escalate or hold the change when the failure mode is data loss or memory corruption you cannot reproduce locally.
